name: CLI Entry Point
description: Click-based CLI providing all subcommands for Ledger: init, backend add, schema add/show/validate, migrate plan/approve, export, mock, and serve. Structured as a Click group hierarchy with nested subgroups (backend, schema, migrate) and top-level commands (init, export, mock, serve). Each subcommand is a thin dispatch layer that loads config, calls the appropriate domain module function via typed Protocols, and formats output for the terminal. Error handling catches LedgerError at the group level and surfaces all violations with severity-colored output to stderr and non-zero exit codes.

types.name: ExitCode
types.description: Process exit codes returned by CLI commands.

types.name: OutputFormat
types.description: Supported output serialization formats for structured command results.

types.name: BackendType
types.description: Supported backend storage types that can be registered with Ledger.

types.name: ExportFormat
types.description: Target contract formats for the export command.

types.name: MockPurpose
types.description: Purpose classification for mock data generation.

types.name: Severity
types.description: Severity level for a validation violation.

types.name: Violation

fields.name: path
fields.description: Dot-delimited path to the offending field or entity, e.g. 'backend.users.columns.email'.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable description of the violation.

fields.name: severity
fields.description: Severity classification of this violation.

fields.name: code
fields.description: Machine-readable error code, e.g. 'SCHEMA_TYPE_MISMATCH'.

types.description: A single validation violation with location, message, and severity.

types.name: LedgerError

fields.name: violations
fields.description: Non-empty list of Violation structs.

validators.error_message: LedgerError must contain at least one violation.

fields.name: exit_code
fields.description: Exit code to use when surfacing this error to the terminal.

types.description: Domain error carrying one or more violations. Raised by domain modules, caught by the CLI group-level error handler.

types.name: CliContext

fields.name: config_path
fields.description: Resolved absolute path to ledger.yaml. Resolution order: --config flag, LEDGER_CONFIG env var, ./ledger.yaml.

fields.name: config
fields.description: Lazy-loaded LedgerConfig from the config component. None until a command decorated with @require_config triggers loading.

fields.name: verbose
fields.description: When true, print debug-level status messages to stderr.

fields.name: output_format
fields.description: Global output format override. Individual commands may support --output to set this.

types.description: Mutable context object threaded through Click commands via @click.pass_context. Implemented as a dataclass (not Pydantic) since it is internal and mutable. Carries resolved config path and lazy-loaded config.

types.name: CommandResult

fields.name: success
fields.description: Whether the command completed without domain errors.

fields.name: data
fields.description: Structured payload for stdout rendering. Type depends on the command.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable status message for stderr.

fields.name: violations
fields.description: Violations to render if success is false.

types.description: Uniform result envelope returned by every thin-dispatch function to the CLI callback for formatting.

types.name: RegistryProtocol

fields.name: register_backend
fields.description: Callable[[str, BackendType, str, any], CommandResult] — registers a backend by id, type, owner component_id, and config.

fields.name: add_schema
fields.description: Callable[[str, any], CommandResult] — ingests a schema YAML file path with config.

fields.name: show_schema
fields.description: Callable[[str, str | None, any], CommandResult] — shows schema for backend_id and optional table name.

fields.name: validate_schemas
fields.description: Callable[[any], CommandResult] — validates all registered schemas against obligations.

types.description: typing.Protocol defining the interface the CLI expects from the registry domain module. Used for dependency injection and testing.

types.name: MigrationProtocol

fields.name: plan_migration
fields.description: Callable[[str, str, any], CommandResult] — creates a migration plan for component_id from migration_file path.

fields.name: approve_migration
fields.description: Callable[[str, str, any], CommandResult] — approves a plan_id with a reviewer id.

types.description: typing.Protocol defining the interface the CLI expects from the migration domain module.

types.name: ExportProtocol

fields.name: export_contracts
fields.description: Callable[[ExportFormat, str | None, any], CommandResult] — exports contracts in target format, optionally filtered by component_id.

types.description: typing.Protocol defining the interface the CLI expects from the export domain module.

types.name: MockProtocol

fields.name: generate_mock_data
fields.description: Callable[[str, str, int, int | None, MockPurpose, any], CommandResult] — generates mock rows for backend_id.table with count, optional seed, purpose, and config.

types.description: typing.Protocol defining the interface the CLI expects from the mock domain module.

types.name: ApiProtocol

fields.name: start_server
fields.description: Callable[[any], None] — starts the FastAPI+uvicorn HTTP server using the provided config.

types.description: typing.Protocol defining the interface the CLI expects from the api domain module.

functions.name: cli_main
functions.description: Root Click group entry point. Resolves config_path (--config flag → LEDGER_CONFIG env var → ./ledger.yaml), initializes CliContext, attaches it to click.Context, and sets up the top-level error handler that catches LedgerError and KeyboardInterrupt.

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: Explicit path to ledger.yaml. If omitted, resolution falls through to env var and CWD.

inputs.name: verbose
inputs.description: Enable verbose debug output to stderr.

inputs.name: output
inputs.description: Global output format for structured commands.

error_cases.name: keyboard_interrupt
error_cases.condition: User sends SIGINT during any subcommand execution

error_cases.name: unhandled_ledger_error
error_cases.condition: A domain module raises LedgerError that propagates to group level

postconditions: CliContext is attached to click.Context.obj
config_path in CliContext is an absolute resolved path string

structured_side_effects.description: Writes colored violation messages and status lines to stderr on error.

functions.name: cmd_init
functions.description: Subcommand `ledger init`. Creates a new ledger.yaml scaffold at the resolved config_path. Does NOT require an existing config (only command that works without @require_config). Delegates to config.init_config.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: CLI context with resolved config_path. config field may be None.

error_cases.name: file_already_exists
error_cases.condition: ledger.yaml already exists at config_path and --force is not set

error_cases.name: write_permission_denied
error_cases.condition: Process lacks write permission to the target directory

postconditions: A valid ledger.yaml file exists at ctx.config_path
Exit code is 0 on success

structured_side_effects.description: Creates a new ledger.yaml scaffold at ctx.config_path.

functions.name: cmd_backend_add
functions.description: Subcommand `ledger backend add <id> --type <type> --owner <component_id>`. Registers a new backend data store. Requires config. Delegates to registry.register_backend.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: CLI context with loaded config.

inputs.name: backend_id
inputs.description: Unique identifier for the backend, e.g. 'users-pg'.

validators.error_message: Backend ID must be lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens/underscores, 1-63 chars, starting with a letter.

inputs.name: backend_type
inputs.description: Storage engine type.

inputs.name: owner
inputs.description: Component ID that owns this backend.

validators.error_message: Owner must be a valid component ID.

error_cases.name: config_not_loaded
error_cases.condition: ledger.yaml not found or unparseable

error_cases.name: duplicate_backend
error_cases.condition: A backend with the same id already exists

error_cases.name: invalid_owner
error_cases.condition: The owner component_id is not registered

preconditions: ctx.config is not None (config loaded via @require_config)
postconditions: Backend is registered in the config
Success message printed to stderr
Exit code is 0

structured_side_effects.description: Prints success or error status message to stderr.

functions.name: cmd_schema_add
functions.description: Subcommand `ledger schema add <path>`. Ingests a schema YAML file verbatim (no normalization or reordering). Requires config. Delegates to registry.add_schema.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: CLI context with loaded config.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: Filesystem path to the schema YAML file to ingest.

error_cases.name: file_not_found
error_cases.condition: The specified path does not exist

error_cases.name: invalid_yaml
error_cases.condition: The file is not valid YAML

error_cases.name: schema_validation_failed
error_cases.condition: The YAML content does not conform to the Ledger schema spec

error_cases.name: config_not_loaded
error_cases.condition: ledger.yaml not found or unparseable

preconditions: path points to an existing readable file
postconditions: Schema is stored verbatim in the registry
Exit code is 0 on success

structured_side_effects.description: Reads schema YAML from the given path for ingestion.
Prints success status or all violations to stderr.

functions.name: cmd_schema_show
functions.description: Subcommand `ledger schema show <backend_id> [<table>]`. Displays the schema for a backend, optionally filtered to a single table. Structured data output to stdout; supports --output json|yaml|text. Requires config. Delegates to registry.show_schema.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: CLI context with loaded config and output_format.

inputs.name: backend_id
inputs.description: Backend identifier to look up.

inputs.name: table
inputs.description: Optional table name to filter output.

error_cases.name: backend_not_found
error_cases.condition: No backend registered with the given id

error_cases.name: table_not_found
error_cases.condition: The specified table does not exist in the backend schema

error_cases.name: config_not_loaded
error_cases.condition: ledger.yaml not found or unparseable

postconditions: Structured data written to stdout in ctx.output_format
Exit code is 0 on success

structured_side_effects.description: Writes structured schema data to stdout in the requested format.

functions.name: cmd_schema_validate
functions.description: Subcommand `ledger schema validate`. Validates all registered schemas against data obligations and annotation rules. Returns ALL violations, not just the first. Structured output to stdout; supports --output. Requires config. Delegates to registry.validate_schemas.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: CLI context with loaded config and output_format.

error_cases.name: no_schemas_registered
error_cases.condition: No schemas have been added to the registry

error_cases.name: config_not_loaded
error_cases.condition: ledger.yaml not found or unparseable

postconditions: All violations are returned (not short-circuited at the first)
Exit code is 0 if no error-severity violations, 1 otherwise
Structured violation list written to stdout

structured_side_effects.description: Writes full violation list to stdout in the requested output format.
Writes summary status line (pass/fail count) to stderr.

functions.name: cmd_migrate_plan
functions.description: Subcommand `ledger migrate plan <component_id> <migration_file>`. Parses a SQL migration file, computes the schema diff, and runs migration gate checks. Returns full violation list with severity levels. Structured output. Requires config. Delegates to migration.plan_migration.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: CLI context with loaded config.

inputs.name: component_id
inputs.description: Component that owns the migration.

validators.error_message: Component ID must be lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens/underscores.

inputs.name: migration_file
inputs.description: Path to the SQL migration file to parse.

error_cases.name: file_not_found
error_cases.condition: The migration file path does not exist

error_cases.name: sql_parse_error
error_cases.condition: The migration file contains unparseable SQL

error_cases.name: component_not_found
error_cases.condition: The component_id is not registered

error_cases.name: gate_violations
error_cases.condition: Migration gate checks produced error-severity violations

error_cases.name: config_not_loaded
error_cases.condition: ledger.yaml not found or unparseable

preconditions: migration_file points to a readable .sql file
postconditions: A migration plan is created with a unique plan_id
All gate violations are returned with severity levels
Exit code is 0 if no error-severity gate violations, 1 otherwise

structured_side_effects.description: Reads the SQL migration file from disk for parsing.
Writes migration plan and gate results to stdout.
Writes summary of gate pass/fail to stderr.

functions.name: cmd_migrate_approve
functions.description: Subcommand `ledger migrate approve <plan_id> --review <id>`. Approves a previously created migration plan with a reviewer identifier. Requires config. Delegates to migration.approve_migration.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: CLI context with loaded config.

inputs.name: plan_id
inputs.description: Unique identifier of the migration plan to approve.

inputs.name: review_id
inputs.description: Identifier of the reviewer approving the plan.

error_cases.name: plan_not_found
error_cases.condition: No migration plan exists with the given plan_id

error_cases.name: plan_already_approved
error_cases.condition: The plan has already been approved

error_cases.name: outstanding_violations
error_cases.condition: The plan has unresolved error-severity gate violations

error_cases.name: config_not_loaded
error_cases.condition: ledger.yaml not found or unparseable

preconditions: A plan with plan_id exists and is in a pending state
postconditions: Plan status is updated to approved
Approval record includes review_id and timestamp
Exit code is 0 on success

structured_side_effects.description: Updates the plan record to approved status with reviewer metadata.
Prints approval confirmation to stderr.

functions.name: cmd_export
functions.description: Subcommand `ledger export --format <pact|arbiter|baton|sentinel> [--component <id>]`. Exports Ledger data as contracts in the target format. Structured data to stdout. Requires config. Delegates to export.export_contracts.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: CLI context with loaded config and output_format.

inputs.name: format
inputs.description: Target contract format to export.

inputs.name: component_id
inputs.description: Optional component ID to filter export scope. If omitted, exports all.

error_cases.name: no_data_to_export
error_cases.condition: No schemas or backends are registered (or none match component filter)

error_cases.name: component_not_found
error_cases.condition: The specified component_id filter does not match any registered component

error_cases.name: config_not_loaded
error_cases.condition: ledger.yaml not found or unparseable

postconditions: Exported contract data written to stdout
Exit code is 0 on success

structured_side_effects.description: Writes exported contract data to stdout in the target format.

functions.name: cmd_mock
functions.description: Subcommand `ledger mock <backend_id> <table> --count N [--seed S] [--purpose canary]`. Generates mock data rows for a table using faker for PII fields and stdlib random for everything else. Structured data to stdout. Requires config. Delegates to mock.generate_mock_data.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: CLI context with loaded config.

inputs.name: backend_id
inputs.description: Backend containing the target table.

inputs.name: table
inputs.description: Table name to generate mock data for.

inputs.name: count
inputs.description: Number of mock rows to generate.

inputs.name: seed
inputs.description: Optional random seed for reproducible output.

inputs.name: purpose
inputs.description: Purpose of the mock data. 'canary' triggers Arbiter registration of generated tokens.

error_cases.name: backend_not_found
error_cases.condition: No backend registered with the given id

error_cases.name: table_not_found
error_cases.condition: The table does not exist in the backend schema

error_cases.name: arbiter_unavailable
error_cases.condition: Purpose is canary but Arbiter service is unreachable

error_cases.name: config_not_loaded
error_cases.condition: ledger.yaml not found or unparseable

postconditions: Exactly `count` rows of mock data are generated
PII fields use faker; non-PII fields use stdlib random
If seed is provided, output is deterministic
Data written to stdout in ctx.output_format

structured_side_effects.description: Writes generated mock data rows to stdout.
When purpose=canary, registers generated canary tokens with the Arbiter service. Skipped with warning if Arbiter is unavailable.

functions.name: cmd_serve
functions.description: Subcommand `ledger serve`. Starts the FastAPI+uvicorn HTTP server exposing the Ledger API. Requires config. Delegates to api.start_server. Blocks until interrupted.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: CLI context with loaded config.

error_cases.name: port_in_use
error_cases.condition: The configured HTTP port is already bound by another process

error_cases.name: config_not_loaded
error_cases.condition: ledger.yaml not found or unparseable

postconditions: Server process is running and accepting HTTP requests
On KeyboardInterrupt, server shuts down cleanly with exit code 130

structured_side_effects.description: Binds and listens on the configured HTTP port, serving FastAPI routes.
Prints server startup banner and access log to stderr.

functions.name: require_config
functions.description: Decorator that ensures CliContext.config is loaded before the wrapped Click callback executes. Resolves config_path, calls config.load_config, and attaches the result to ctx.config. Raises LedgerError with exit_code CONFIG_ERROR_3 on failure.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: CLI context whose config field will be populated.

error_cases.name: config_file_missing
error_cases.condition: No file exists at ctx.config_path

error_cases.name: config_parse_error
error_cases.condition: The file exists but contains invalid YAML

error_cases.name: config_validation_error
error_cases.condition: YAML is valid but does not conform to LedgerConfig schema

postconditions: ctx.config is a valid LedgerConfig instance (not None)
Config was parsed from the YAML file at ctx.config_path

structured_side_effects.description: Reads and parses the ledger.yaml configuration file from disk.

functions.name: format_output
functions.description: Pure helper that serializes a CommandResult's data payload into the requested OutputFormat string for writing to stdout. Handles text (human-readable tables/indented), json, and yaml formats.

inputs.name: result
inputs.description: The command result whose data field will be serialized.

inputs.name: output_format
inputs.description: Target serialization format.

error_cases.name: unserializable_data
error_cases.condition: result.data contains types that cannot be serialized to the target format

preconditions: result.data is not None when format is json or yaml
postconditions: Returned string is valid JSON if output_format is json
Returned string is valid YAML if output_format is yaml
Returned string is human-readable if output_format is text

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function with no side effects.

functions.name: render_violations
functions.description: Pure helper that formats a list of Violations into a colored, human-readable string for stderr output. Groups violations by severity and includes counts.

inputs.name: violations
inputs.description: List of Violation structs to render.

inputs.name: use_color
inputs.description: Whether to include ANSI color codes in output. Disabled when stderr is not a TTY.

postconditions: All violations are included in the output (none skipped)
Violations are grouped by severity: errors first, then warnings, then info
Output includes a summary line with counts per severity

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function with no side effects.

invariants: Each CLI subcommand performs zero business logic — it only dispatches to the corresponding domain module function.
Data output goes to stdout; status messages and errors go to stderr.
Config resolution order is always: --config flag → LEDGER_CONFIG env var → ./ledger.yaml.
LedgerError violations are never truncated — all violations are rendered to the user.
Exit codes are deterministic: 0=success, 1=domain/validation error, 2=usage error (Click default), 3=config error, 130=KeyboardInterrupt.
Schema YAML is stored verbatim — the CLI never normalizes, reformats, or reorders YAML content.
The cmd_init function is the only command that operates without a loaded config.
When Arbiter is unreachable during canary mock generation, the CLI warns on stderr and continues — it never crashes.
All Click callbacks are annotated with return type None.
Each CLI source file stays under 300 lines; the cli/ subpackage has one file per command group.

data_access.rationale: Reads ledger.yaml configuration file (INTERNAL tier) to resolve config_path, backend definitions, and schema paths. The init command writes a new ledger.yaml scaffold (INTERNAL tier). No PII or PUBLIC data is directly read or written by the CLI layer; PII handling is delegated to the mock component.

side_effects.rationale: Reads ledger.yaml from disk to populate CliContext.config for all commands except init.
The init command writes a new ledger.yaml scaffold file to the resolved config path.
All data output (show, validate, plan, export) is written to stdout for piping.
Violations, warnings, and status messages are written to stderr to separate from data output.
